chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory When an Animal Strikes a Person with Its Front or Hind Legs

text: رِوَايَةِ غِيَاثِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ضَمَّنَ صَاحِبَ اَلدَّابَّةِ مَا وَطِئَتْ بِيَدَيْهَا وَ مَا نَفَحَتْ بِرِجْلَيْهَا فَلاَ ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَضْرِبَهَا إِنْسَانٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5353 - In the narration of Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) held the owner of the animal liable for what it trampled with its front legs and what it struck with its hind legs. However, there is no liability upon him unless a person strikes the animal."
